HML Devil Factors are long high value stocks and short low value stocks, based on book to market values, using the current total market value of equity .
Here we construct BAB factors for U.S. equities and 23 international equity markets, as well as multiple aggregates.

The portfolios are an updated and extended version of the equity portfolios used in Asness and Frazzini (2013), "The Devil in HML‛s Details," Journal of Portfolio Management, 39, 49-68.
The portfolios are an updated and extended version of the equity portfolios used in Frazzini, Andrea and Lasse H. Pedersen (2014), “Betting Against Beta," Journal of Financial Economics, 111, 1–25.
This file contains monthly U.S. Treasury bill rates.
This file contains monthly market returns in excess of t-bills.
This file contains monthly self-financing excess returns of long/short equity Betting Against Beta (BAB) factors.
This file contains monthly self-financing returns of equity High Minus Low (HML) factors using the Book to Market definition of Fama and French (1992).
This file contains monthly self-financing returns of equity High Minus Low Devil (HML Devil) factors using the Book to Market definition of Asness and Frazzini (2013).
This file contains monthly self-financing returns of equity Small Minus Big (SMB) factors.
This file contains monthly self-financing returns of equity Up Minus Down (UMD) factors.
We construct HML Devil factors for U.S. equities and 23 international equity markets, as well as multiple aggregates.
